p1: All donations are received into programme-specific sub-accounts

maintained at the Foundation’s bank. Funds donated for a specific programme — for example, ‘Vaccine Reminders’ — are legally and operationally separated from the general corpus and can only be released for that designated purpose.
wallets.p2: This architecture is disclosed to donors at the point of giving, published in our quarterly reports, and verified annually by our statutory auditor.
p3: Undisbursed programme funds at year-end are carried forward and ring-fenced for the following year.
No surplus is absorbed into administration
without Board resolution and public disclosure.

pledges.title: The Accountability Pledge
pledges.subtitle: Seven binding commitments we make to every donor.
items: We will never spend more than 30% of total donations on administrative, technology, and overhead costs combined.
We will publish audited accounts within 90 days of each financial year end.
We will publish every Board resolution summary within 30 days of each meeting.
We will issue 80G certificates automatically within 2 minutes — no requests, no delays, no exceptions.
We will respond to every donor query within 48 working hours.
We will publicly acknowledge, in writing, when we fail to meet any programme target — along with an explanation and a corrective plan.
We will never redirect designated programme funds to general operations without donor notification.

quote.line1: "Transparency is not a report we file once a year.
quote.line2: It is a culture we practise every day
quote.line4: with every person who trusts us."
quote.author: Sowjanya, Founder
